author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
Perseverance is Given to the End.
title: Perseverance is Given to the End.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: Rightly follows the word to the kingdom of the elect: “If God be for us, who can be against us?

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: He that spared not His own Son, but delivered Him up for us all, how has He not also with Him given us all things?

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: Who shall lay anything to the charge of God’s elect?

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: Christ who died? yea, rather who rose again also, who is at the right hand of God, who also soliciteth on our behalf?”

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: And of how stedfast a perseverance even to the end they have received the gift, let them follow on to say: “Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: As it is written, Because for thy sake we are killed all the day long, we are accounted as sheep for the slaughter.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: But in all these things we are more than conquerors, through Him that hath loved us.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: For I am certain, that neither death, nor life, nor angel, nor principality, nor things present, nor things to come, nor power, nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature, shall be able to separate us from the love of God which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.”
